briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — finesse spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for spawn with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Switch to Ned rig (1/4-oz head, same baits as dropshot) when smallmouth are going down and nipping the dropshot weight instead of the bait

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Switch to Ned rig (1/4-oz head, same baits as dropshot) when smallmouth are going down and nipping the dropshot weight instead of the bait
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, cover. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In stained water, use a chartreuse jig head on the traditional Ned rig for added visual edge with a 3-inch stick bait.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, cover. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Yamamoto pro Fukae: Uses 14-lb YGK sinking braid to an 8-lb fluorocarbon leader for spawn-time drop-shot and Ned Rig presentations on a Shimano Expride 170M with 2500-size spinning reel

Wheeler: Smallmouth spawn starts in creeks and flat areas with no current, then they transition toward the main river/lake — same as largemouth. Look for spawners in 10-15 ft rather than the obvious 4-8 ft most people target
Hackney: 'At Stick Marsh with no underwater vegetation, fish were spawning around willow trees and cane on the shoreline - they just wanted to have something to spawn around.'

Johnston: Developed the SPRO CJ Smasher as a versatile 3-inch worm that is durable (Dura Tuff material), scent-infused (Amino Bite), and can be rigged multiple ways including drop shot, shakey head, Ned rig, and Texas rig.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal spawning bass become extremely lockjaw but won't abandon beds. Fish are still in 2-5ft but much harder to trigger. Slow way down and make repeated presentations to visible beds.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
